reviews.author: Jeroen De Koninck
reviews.text: Fine beach but as everything a bit dirty and unnecessary ugly constructions (pipe) across the beach. Don’t pay for parking, it’s a scam. We just parked our bike and refused to pay as it’s not official at all.

reviews.author: Andrew “Andy” Davis
reviews.text: Ride out past paddy fields and to the beach. Soak in the waves and relax and go get a coffee and some food. Nice way to spend a Sunday. Sad to see the flood barriers which can change the look of photos but obviously necessary after recent history.

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: Cua Dai Beach has the foundations of a tropical paradise—with soft sand, tall palm trees, and long stretches of thatched umbrellas offering shade and a peaceful ocean view. From a distance, it definitely has that classic postcard look and a relaxing vibe, especially if you’re just looking to lie on a sunbed and enjoy the breeze. However, a closer look reveals that the area has seen better days. Many of the beachfront restaurants and buildings seem quite tired and in need of maintenance, and the surrounding grounds—especially the patches of overgrown weeds and grass—reflect years of neglect, likely since the pandemic. It’s a shame, because the location has a lot of potential to be a premier coastal destination. Still worth a visit if you’re nearby and looking for some quiet beach time, but it feels like a place that’s been somewhat forgotten and is waiting for a proper revival.

reviews.author: Keith Sim
reviews.text: Really peaceful, and quiet beach. There are few people and the beach is beautiful.. white sandy beaches much better than those at A Bang Beach. Lounges and umbrellas are available along the beaches at a fee. I paid about VND 50k to use a lounger which is highly recommended if you do not want to be roasted by the scorching sun. Toilets are available for shower. In my opinion, can just skip An Bang beach and come here instead if you want to avoid the crowds.

reviews.author: Sud K
reviews.text: Peaceful and secluded beach, very much preferable to An Bang beach. There are no shacks there but a few shops that sell coconut water etc. Parking was 10000 vnd. If you want to have a quiet time by yourself this is the place to be. There’s no one to bother or disturb you and the water is crystal clear, the beach is clean too.

reviews.author: Bridget Morris
reviews.text: This was a great little find, we only went because there was a shuttle from our hotel. It’s a beautiful, long stretch of beach with soft white sand and blue water. We didn’t swim but the water was lovely when we walked along the edge. There were lots of beachside restaurants to chose from, none of them fancy but the food and drinks were still good and you can rent lounges on the sand.

reviews.author: Tweetu Piyudi
reviews.text: We took a Grab Taxi from An Bang Beach, where we spent 4 hours. This beach was much less crowded on 10th Nov '24, with only a couple of tourists. The sea water appeared cleaner and greener here, but food options were limited. For those seeking a private experience, this is an excellent beach. However, if you prefer a crowd during the off-season, An Bang Beach is ideal. Remember to carry your own drinking water.

reviews.author: Gunaserlan Batumelae
reviews.text: Clean and nice beach with cafes to relax

reviews.author: J Ryan
reviews.text: A very long beach so all different kinds of areas, restaurants, layout spots...etc. I found a great spot for me away from the hustle and bustle where I could play my own music and have a personal server for food and drink. It costs me $20,000 Dong ($1.00 usd) for my own layout chair and umbrella (unlimited hours). A killer deal considering most places charge $60,000-$80,000 Dong an hour. Had 3 drinks and a Banh Mi Sandwich for the equivalent of $5.00 usd. I'll be back in two days. Edit: Guess it's a nude beach as well because this ridiculously good looking woman was laying out with no top on. +++ in my book!

reviews.author: Hrvoje Petrovic
reviews.text: Beautiful beach, still not invaded by mass tourism like you can see in Thailand. Sea is very wavy, not turquoise blue, if you swimm don't go too far from the shore. I love this place and whole area, very relaxed, just go little bit more from the place where is the main toad to Hoi An. As you can see on that particular place are tones big sand bags, I guess some kind of protection from tsunami wave. So when you reach it, go left, and with bicycle you need additional 10minutes to reach Oppio bar where is good long sandy beach without those bags.

reviews.author: Gregory Zeleny
reviews.text: Getting better and better every year — the beach is quiet, peaceful and relaxed. It is mostly local restaurants — and pretty much all seafood. They have put some new signs and displays up to make the beach look more beautiful. 🤩 Great work!

reviews.author: Sue Noplaneboss
reviews.text: Lovely sandy beach with chairs and some shops and cafe joined up to Hidden beach.

reviews.author: r41nm4k3r _
reviews.text: we visited in mid November and the weather was pleasant. the beach was deserted, but it was mesmerizing to enjoy the seclusion. perfect for romantic beach-side walks. no restaurants on or around the beach. free parking is available just besides the beach.

reviews.author: Dave Welsh traveller
reviews.text: Clean beach with a few restaurants and sunbeds

reviews.author: Robert Sixtyone
reviews.text: In 2025, rebuilding sea walls and break waters continues. Looks in much better shape than An Bang beach. Restaurants and hotels here, probably better move. Was different in 2024, but An Bang beach suffered over winter. Hope for improvements to and past An Bang beach pre summer 2026.

reviews.author: madhu chadha
reviews.text: Not so crowded Shallow to a point, so safe for kids Nice views and quiter than most beaches

reviews.author: darren hargreaves
reviews.text: Not as busy as Bn Bang so a peaceful beach only 5000 to park your bike

reviews.author: Feng Nguyen
reviews.text: The most beautiful beach in Hoi An is now back!!

reviews.author: Arnau Farré
reviews.text: Very nice. Clear water and nice views

reviews.author: Maria Bessa
reviews.text: Wonderful warm water a few restaurants with fresh sea food just the beach needs a little bit cleaning 😀

reviews.author: Tassilo Kubitz
reviews.text: The beach is only held together with breakwaters. This is not really a beach.

reviews.author: Thiều Nguyễn
reviews.text: The beach is very clean. Great place to watch sunrise as well as sunset...

reviews.author: Duy “THÀNH DUY” Thành
reviews.text: Cua Dai Beach In Summer. Romantic and clean

reviews.author: Pat Beck
reviews.text: This is a good beach but it.was recently washed away in 2014. There are loads of giant sand bags to try to rebuild the sandy beach. The palms are nice for shade but the vendors try to force you out or place chairs directly in front of people who do not want to buy their over priced goods.

reviews.author: PandaPaw P
reviews.text: My favorite place when I'm in the area to chill with cold beer away from the crowds of Hoi An.

reviews.author: Frankie Koo
reviews.text: If you love Surfing , you will love this beach

reviews.author: Jonny Cavallo
reviews.text: Nice and quiet beach ⛱️ It usually gets a bit windy, so prepare to be covered in sand 😅

reviews.author: Ak
reviews.text: Just an intersection nearby the beach.
